No, 'PTO' does not commonly stand for paid time on; it stands for paid time off.

No, the printing press existed before the modern internet. It was invented in the 15th century, while the internet emerged in the late 20th century.
World War II ended in the 20th century.
No, the Roman Empire did not exist before the Industrial Revolution; it existed long before it, falling in the 5th century AD while the Industrial Revolution began in the 18th century.
No, APR stands for Annual Percentage Rate, not Annual Payment Rate.
No, that is incorrect. If you consistently spend more than you earn, you are running a deficit. A surplus occurs when you earn more than you spend.
No, money kept in a fireproof box at home does not earn interest by itself; it requires a financial institution or account to generate interest.
No, water boils at a lower temperature at high altitude than at sea level.
No, baking soda and baking powder are not the exact same ingredient. Baking soda is pure sodium bicarbonate, while baking powder contains baking soda plus an acidifying agent and a drying agent.
No, raw chicken should never share a cutting board with ready-to-eat salad without washing it, as this can cause cross-contamination and lead to foodborne illness.
No, in the United States, cars normally drive on the right side of the road. Your teacher is incorrect.
No, one kilometer is shorter than one mile. One mile is approximately 1.609 kilometers.
No, a magnetic compass needle points toward magnetic north. The north-seeking pole of the needle is attracted to the Earth's magnetic north pole, which is located near the geographic North Pole.

No, pressing Ctrl+S typically saves the current document, not prints it.

No, a folder is not the same thing as a single file. A folder (or directory) is a container used to organize and group multiple files and subfolders, whereas a file is a specific unit of data stored in the file system.

While flashcards are a powerful tool for many, they are not universally superior to other strategies like active recall, spaced repetition with varied materials, or teaching concepts to others.
